stories.title: D11 $775 Million Bond Referendum — What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: D11 parents: the school board is considering a $775M bond on the November ballot. Average school building is 58 years old. Repairs needed exceed $422M. Voters decide in November. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The D11 $775M bond would raise property taxes by ~$6.50/month per $100K of home value. It would fund AC for aging schools, building renovations, and teacher pay. November ballot. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

COS parents — there's a big school funding question coming to your November ballot, and now is the time to get informed.

The D11 board is considering a $775 million bond referendum for the November 2026 election. If voters approve it, the money would go toward:

- Air conditioning upgrades (many D11 schools are 50–100 years old and have none)
- Major building renovations
- A boost to teacher pay

Here's the bottom line on cost: your property taxes would go up by about $6.50 per month for every $100,000 of home value. The mill levy would increase from 37.7 to 46.3 mills — which would put D11 on par with surrounding districts.

For context: the average D11 building is 58 years old. Repair needs already identified exceed $422 million. This isn't a wish list — it's long-deferred maintenance.

Nothing is finalized. The board is still evaluating whether to put it on the ballot. But as a parent, this is worth following.

What questions do you have about the D11 bond?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM MT

D11 families: a $775M bond could hit your November ballot. It would fund AC, renovations, and teacher pay — with a tax impact of ~$6.50/month per $100K home value. Here's what you need to know.

article.headline: D11 $775 Million Bond Referendum: What Colorado Springs Parents Need to Know Before November

stories.title: PPLD Summer Adventure Launches June 2 — Free Reading Program and Prizes for All Ages

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: PPLD Summer Adventure starts Tuesday, June 2. Free for all ages. Register at ppld.beanstack.org or any library branch. Complete 30 days of activities to win prizes from SCHEELS, Switchbacks, Flying W Ranch + more. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Free lunches for kids and teens 18 and under this summer at PPLD East Library (5550 N. Union Blvd.), May 27–July 31, weekdays at 11 AM. No sign-up needed. Summer Adventure reading program also starts June 2. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

Summer is officially here, and so is one of the best free programs in Colorado Springs.

The Pikes Peak Library District's Summer Adventure kicks off this Tuesday, June 2 — and it's open to every age, from babies to grandparents.

Here's how it works:
- Register at any PPLD library branch or at ppld.beanstack.org (free)
- Complete 30 days of reading, creating, or exploring activities
- Earn prizes: you get one on registration day (ages 0–18), another after 30 days, and entries into grand prize drawings for additional activity days

Grand prizes include gift cards and experiences from Roadhouse Cinemas, Colorado Springs Switchbacks, SCHEELS, Skate City, Main Event, and Flying W Ranch.

Opening day (June 2) features free concerts at East Library and Library 21c — four concerts total at 2 PM and 3:30 PM at both locations.

BONUS: Free lunches for kids and teens 18 and under are available weekdays at East Library (5550 N. Union Blvd.) through July 31. No sign-up needed. Adults can grab lunch for $5.

Is your family signing up for Summer Adventure this year?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:00 AM MT

PPLD Summer Adventure starts Tuesday, June 2. Free for all ages. Prizes, free lunches, opening day concerts. Register at ppld.beanstack.org or any library branch. Summer is officially here.

stories.title: CPSC Warning — Fasando Baby Loungers Linked to Infant Death: Stop Using Immediately

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: URGENT: CPSC warns to stop using Fasando baby loungers (Amazon). Infant death reported 2025. ~6,200 units sold. Cut cover in half and dispose. Visit CPSC.gov. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Fasando baby lounger warning: sides too low, openings too wide, no stand. Hazards present even on elevated surfaces. Manufacturer has not issued a recall. If you have one: remove padding, cut cover in half, dispose. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

This is an urgent safety post. Please share with any parent you know who has a newborn or infant at home.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has issued a warning about Fasando baby loungers sold on Amazon — linked to the death of a 2-month-old in 2025. About 6,200 units were sold between August 2024 and July 2025, for approximately $35.

The problem: the lounger's sides are too low, the foot openings are too wide, and there's no stand — meaning if placed on an elevated surface like an adult bed, there's a serious fall and entrapment risk.

IMPORTANT: The manufacturer has not agreed to a formal recall. That means these products are still in homes.

If you have one:
1. Stop using it immediately
2. Remove the foam padding
3. Cut the cover in half
4. Dispose of it

For more information or to report an issue: CPSC.gov or call 800-638-2772.

Please share this post. A product linked to an infant death is still circulating.

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 12:00 PM MT

URGENT: CPSC warning on Fasando baby loungers sold on Amazon — linked to an infant death in 2025. No formal recall yet. If you have one: stop using it, cut the cover in half, dispose of it. CPSC.gov for details.

article.headline: CPSC Warns Families to Stop Using Fasando Baby Loungers Immediately — Infant Death Reported

stories.title: D49 Student Success Center Opens August 2026 — What Families Need to Know

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: D49 families: the new Student Success Center opens August 2026 in the former Falcon Elementary building. Mon–Thu preschool classes, Base49 care. Targeted support for students with specialized needs. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM MT

D49 families — a new resource is coming to your district this August, and it includes a preschool option worth knowing about.

District 49 is opening a Student Success Center at the start of the 2026–27 school year. It will be housed in the former Falcon Elementary School of Technology building.

What will be there:
- Two full-time preschool classrooms, available Monday through Thursday
- Before- and after-school care provided by Base49
- Friday childcare through Base49
- Specialized support services for students who need extra help

The center is designed as a strategic realignment — consolidating services into one location so students with specialized needs can get targeted support.

If you have a preschool-age child or a student who might benefit from the support services, keep an eye on d49.org for enrollment and program details as the summer progresses.

D49 parents — are you planning to use any of these services this fall?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:00 PM MT

D49 families: a new Student Success Center opens August 2026 with Mon–Thu preschool classes, Base49 care, and specialized student services. Find out more at d49.org.

stories.title: Free June Events for Colorado Springs Families

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Free June fun in COS: El Pomar Family Day June 6 (10 AM–2 PM), free Bancroft Park concerts Tuesdays at 6 PM, Old Colorado City events Thursdays at 4 PM. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 5:00 PM MT

June in Colorado Springs is full of free things to do with your family — and we've rounded up the best ones.

Here's what's on:

El Pomar Family Exploration Day — June 6, 10 AM–2 PM at the Penrose Heritage Museum. Free and open to the public.

Free Bancroft Park Concerts — Tuesday nights, June through October, 6–8 PM. Grab a blanket and enjoy outdoor music under the Colorado sky.

First & Main Town Center Live Music — Every Friday in June and July, 5–7 PM. Local bands, outdoor setting, family-friendly.

Old Colorado City Thursdays — Every Thursday in June, 4–9 PM. Music, food, and community in one of COS's most beloved neighborhoods.

Colorado College Summer Music Festival — June 6–26. Performances from 52 advanced student musicians from around the country.

And of course: PPLD Summer Adventure starts June 2 with prizes, free lunches, and opening day concerts.

What's your family most excited about this June in Colorado Springs?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 5:00 PM MT

Free in COS this June: El Pomar Family Day (June 6), free Bancroft Park concerts every Tuesday, Old Colorado City Thursdays, First & Main Fridays, and PPLD Summer Adventure starting June 2. Summer is here.

article.headline: Free June Events for Colorado Springs Families: Concerts, Museums, and Summer Fun
